section_label: Special Domains — Military, Education, Ethnicity/Language, Environment & Remaining Equality Variables

variables.description: Whether the military or armed forces are mentioned in the constitution
Who is the commander in chief of the armed forces (asked only if MILITARY=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Head of State; 2=Head of Government; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable
variables.notes: Civilian commander-in-chief (Head of State or Government) is a key indicator of civilian supremacy over the military

variables.description: Whether there is special mention of terrorism and public security provisions regarding terrorism (asked only if MILITARY=1)
Whether the constitution refers to a duty of military service
variables.notes: Borderline; conscription duties can indicate the state's relationship between civilians and military service

variables.description: Whether the constitution contains provisions concerning national integration of ethnic communities
Whether the constitution provides for positive obligations to transfer wealth to, or provide opportunity for, particular groups (affirmative action provisions)
Whether the constitution specifies either an official or national language
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Official only; 2=National only; 3=Both official and national; 4=No official/national language but language specified for government business; 5=No languages mentioned; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine
variables.notes: Relevant to minority rights when multiple language communities exist

variables.description: Whether the constitution refers to the protection of different languages
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 3=Constitution explicitly requires neutrality towards languages; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution refers to protection or preservation of the environment
variables.notes: Borderline; environmental rights are increasingly recognized as related to socioeconomic well-being

variables.description: How the constitution refers to the environment (asked only if ENV=1). Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): ENVREF_1=Duty of the State to Protect; ENVREF_2=Duty of the People to Protect; ENVREF_3=Right of the People to Enjoy; ENVREF_4=General Reference; ENVREF_90=Left to non-constitutional law; ENVREF_96=Other; ENVREF_97=Unable to Determine; ENVREF_98=Not Specified
variables.notes: ENVREF_3 (right to enjoy environment) is most relevant to civil/socioeconomic rights; ENVREF_1 and ENVREF_2 reflect duties

variables.description: Whether the constitution contains provisions concerning education
Whether the constitution stipulates that education be compulsory until at least some level (asked only if EDUCATE=1)
To what level the constitution makes education compulsory (asked only if EDCOMP=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Basic/elementary/primary; 2=Secondary/intermediate; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution stipulates that education be free, at least up to some level (must reference education at no cost, not merely public education)
To what level the constitution stipulates education should be free (asked only if EDFREE=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Basic/elementary/primary; 2=Secondary/intermediate; 3=All levels; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution guarantees academic freedom
Whether the constitution guarantees equal access to higher education
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=Yes, but qualified (access limited by ability or competitive basis); 3=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution refers to a state duty to protect or promote culture or cultural rights (must specifically mention 'culture' or 'cultural rights')
How the constitution addresses the state operation of print or electronic media
variables.coding_scheme: 1=State must operate all media outlets; 2=State can operate media outlets; 3=State cannot operate media outlets; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified

variables.description: General principles about the operation of the media market mentioned in the constitution. Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): MEDMARK_1=No monopoly or oligopoly; MEDMARK_2=Competitive; MEDMARK_3=Pluralism; MEDMARK_4=Balanced; MEDMARK_5=Fair; MEDMARK_6=Only monopoly; MEDMARK_7=Oligopoly; MEDMARK_90=Left to non-constitutional law; MEDMARK_96=Other; MEDMARK_97=Unable to Determine; MEDMARK_98=Not Specified
